refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link under agreed conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency guarantees.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the vehicle to reduce network hops.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams via QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and configuration.
Interaction with safety services for latency monitoring.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates teleoperation communication protocol design, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with existing vehicle communication stacks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Implements teleoperation communication protocols and manages end-to-end latency.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle status data to the operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of teleoperation latency logic; low diffusion as protocols are centralized.
Vehicle module handles local control but latency protocol logic is minimal here.
Safety services monitor latency but do not implement communication protocols.
Overall system coordination but not focused on latency protocol implementation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation latency logic in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces poorly with Vehicle module.
Risk of interface bottlenecks on Network Link if not properly managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface performance and consider co-locating latency-sensitive components if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated low-latency protocols) directly addresses the refined FR sub-requirements with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via Network Link and Telemetry interfaces, enabling clear verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with well-defined interfaces and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance closely during integration testing.
Validate interface throughput and latency under realistic conditions.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and verifiable with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with latency guarantees.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation protocols can be integrated with existing vehicle systems.
open_risks: Potential network link bottlenecks impacting latency.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under test network conditions.
Integration test results between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
